# Machine Learning L2

> Apply ML to investment problems — supervised (penalised regression, SVM, KNN, CART, random forest), unsupervised (PCA, clustering), neural networks, deep learning, reinforcement learning

**Category:** Trading · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** quant · machine-learning · supervised · unsupervised · neural-networks · cfa-level-2

## What this does

Apply ML to investment problems — supervised (penalised regression, SVM, KNN, CART, random forest), unsupervised (PCA, clustering), neural networks, deep learning, reinforcement learning

Specifies 2 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **model_id** *(text, required)* — Model identifier
- **algorithm** *(select, required)* — penalised_regression | svm | knn | cart | random_forest | pca | kmeans | hierarchical | neural_network | deep_learning | reinforcement

## What must be true

- **supervised_vs_unsupervised → supervised:** Labelled data; predict Y from X (regression or classification)
- **supervised_vs_unsupervised → unsupervised:** No labels; discover structure (clusters, components)
- **generalisation_overfitting → bias_variance:** High bias = underfit; high variance = overfit
- **generalisation_overfitting → train_validate_test:** Partition; train on training, tune on validation, evaluate on test
- **generalisation_overfitting → k_fold_cv:** K-fold cross-validation reduces variance of performance estimate
- **prevention_overfitting → regularisation:** Penalise model complexity (L1/L2)
- **prevention_overfitting → early_stopping:** Halt training when validation error rises
- **penalised_regression → lasso:** L1 penalty; performs variable selection by zeroing coefficients
- **penalised_regression → ridge:** L2 penalty; shrinks coefficients without zeroing
- **penalised_regression → elastic_net:** Combines L1 and L2 penalties
- **svm:** Linear classifier maximising margin between classes; kernel trick handles non-linear
- **knn:** Classify by majority vote of k nearest neighbours; sensitive to feature scaling
- **cart:** Recursive partitioning of feature space; prone to overfitting without pruning
- **ensemble → bagging:** Bootstrap aggregating; reduces variance
- **ensemble → random_forest:** Bagging on decision trees with random feature subsets at each split
- **ensemble → boosting:** Sequential weak learners weighted by previous errors
- **unsupervised → pca:** Linear orthogonal transformation maximising variance per component; eigenvalue scree plot to choose k
- **unsupervised → kmeans:** Partition into k clusters minimising within-cluster sum of squares
- **unsupervised → hierarchical:** Agglomerative or divisive; produces dendrogram
- **neural_networks → architecture:** Input layer → hidden layers (with non-linear activation) → output layer
- **neural_networks → backpropagation:** Gradient descent on loss function via chain rule
- **neural_networks → deep_learning:** Many hidden layers; learns hierarchical representations
- **reinforcement_learning → framework:** Agent learns policy maximising cumulative reward via state-action-reward feedback
- **reinforcement_learning → application:** Algorithmic trading strategies that adapt online
- **validation → model_required:** model_id present
- **validation → valid_algorithm:** algorithm in allowed set

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Train Ml Model** — when model_id exists; algorithm in ["penalised_regression","svm","knn","cart","random_forest","pca","kmeans","hierarchical","neural_network","deep_learning","reinforcement"], then call service; emit ml.trained. _Why: Train ML model._

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Invalid Algorithm** — when algorithm not_in ["penalised_regression","svm","knn","cart","random_forest","pca","kmeans","hierarchical","neural_network","deep_learning","reinforcement"], then emit ml.train_rejected. _Why: Unsupported algorithm._ *(error: `ML_INVALID_ALGORITHM`)*

## Errors it can return

- `ML_INVALID_ALGORITHM` — algorithm must be one of the supported ML algorithms

## Events

**`ml.trained`**
  Payload: `model_id`, `algorithm`, `train_score`, `validation_score`, `hyperparameters`

**`ml.train_rejected`**
  Payload: `model_id`, `reason_code`

## Connects to

- **machine-learning-techniques** *(required)*
- **big-data-projects-l2** *(recommended)*
